title: Basketball Match (FIBA layout)
description: FIBA / international scoresheet. Layout doc: extraction-prompt-fiba.txt. Page: L = L1 Team A, L2 Team B, L3 officials; R = R1 running score, R2 period scores, R3 final. Header (full width): row 1 = competition, date, time, referee; row 2 = game number, place, umpires 1 & 2. L1/L2 player table columns: Licence No., Player name, Kit No., Player In (×4), Fouls (×5). L3 order on sheet: scorekeeper, assistant scorekeeper, timekeeper, 24s, referee, umpires, captain signature. R1: four 40-point blocks typical (plays 1–160); pointsPerColumn 40 or 60. For Ireland domestic sheets use schema.json + extraction-prompt.txt.

time.description: Time of the match in 24-hour format (HH:MM)

referee2.description: Second official (FIBA: 'Umpire 1'). L3 may repeat names.

gameNumber.description: FIBA: Game No. from the header.

umpire2Name.description: FIBA: 'Umpire 2' (third court official). Use referee1=Referee, referee2=Umpire 1, umpire2Name=Umpire 2.

assistantScorekeeperName.description: FIBA: Assistant Scorekeeper (when listed separately from Scorekeeper).

teamAColour.description: L1: Team A kit colour after 'Colour' (team header block).

teamBColour.description: L2: Team B kit colour after 'Colour'.

teamACoachName.description: L1 staff row: head coach name below player table.

teamACoachBipin.description: L1: coach licence / BIPIN (FIBA: 'Licence no.' beside Coach).

teamAAssistantCoachName.description: L1: assistant coach name (often no BIPIN).

l3Officials.description: L3: officials table  -  referee 1 & 2, scorekeeper, time keeper, 24s operator; captain signature row may have no BIPIN.

role.description: One of: referee1, referee2, scorekeeper, assistantScorekeeper, timekeeper, shotClock24s, captainSignature

bipinNo.description: Official BIPIN; empty for captain signature if no column.

teamATimeoutsFirstHalf.description: Team A time-outs used in the first half. Check each box: slash (/), backslash (\), or horizontal line (-) = one time-out; blank = not used. First half has 2 boxes. Sum to get total (0-2).

teamATimeoutsSecondHalf.description: Team A time-outs used in the second half. Same box notation: /, \, or - = used; blank = not used. Second half has 3 boxes. Sum to get total (0-3).

teamATimeoutsExtraPeriods.description: Team A time-outs used in extra period(s). Each extra period has 3 boxes (/, \, - = used; blank = not used). If no slashes or lines in extra-period boxes, game had no extra periods (use 0). Sum all extra-period boxes to get total.

teamAFoulsPeriod1.description: Team A team fouls in period 1. Four sequential boxes per period. Count ONLY a slash (/) or backslash (\) as one team foul. A horizontal dash (– or -) or a blank box means NO foul in that slot; do not count dashes. Integer 0–4 = number of / or \ marks only. If all four boxes show / or \, use 4 (sheet may cap at four marks; bonus rules still apply on court).

teamAFoulsPeriod2.description: Team A team fouls in period 2. Same counting: only / or \ count as fouls; dash or blank = not a foul (0–4).

teamAFoulsPeriod3.description: Team A team fouls in period 3. Same: count only / or \; dash or blank = 0 toward count (0–4).

teamAFoulsPeriod4.description: Team A team fouls in period 4. Same: count only / or \; dash or blank = not a foul (0–4).

teamBTimeoutsFirstHalf.description: Team B time-outs used in the first half. Check each box: /, \, or - = one time-out; blank = not used. First half has 2 boxes. Sum to get total (0-2).

teamBTimeoutsSecondHalf.description: Team B time-outs used in the second half. Same box notation: /, \, - = used; blank = not used. Second half has 3 boxes. Sum to get total (0-3).

teamBTimeoutsExtraPeriods.description: Team B time-outs used in extra period(s). Each extra period has 3 boxes. If no slashes or lines in extra-period boxes, use 0. Sum all extra-period boxes to get total.

teamBFoulsPeriod1.description: Team B team fouls in period 1. Four boxes per period. Count ONLY / or \ as one foul each. Dash (– or -) or blank = no foul in that slot (do not count). Integer 0–4.

teamBFoulsPeriod2.description: Team B team fouls in period 2. Same: only / or \ count; dash or blank = not a foul (0–4).

teamBFoulsPeriod3.description: Team B team fouls in period 3. Same: only / or \ count; dash or blank = not a foul (0–4).

teamBFoulsPeriod4.description: Team B team fouls in period 4. Same: only / or \ count; dash or blank = not a foul (0–4).

teamAPlayers.description: L1 player table (home / Team A). One object per printed roster row. Column order on sheet: Licence No., Player name, Kit No., Player In Q1–Q4, Fouls ×5 (maps to bipinNo, playerName, kitNo, playerInQuarter1–4, foul1–5). Up to 12 rows typical. Exclude coach rows.

bipinNo.description: Player licence / BIPIN / PIN (FIBA: Licence no. column).

playerInQuarter1.description: Player In Q1: X, /, or \ = played; empty = did not play. Empty boxes may mean not filled out; scorekeepers often mark only starters.

playerInQuarter2.description: Player In Q2: X, /, or \ = played; empty = did not play.

playerInQuarter3.description: Player In Q3: X, /, or \ = played; empty = did not play.

playerInQuarter4.description: Player In Q4: X, /, or \ = played; empty = did not play.

playerName.description: Player name (any format as on sheet).

foul1.description: Foul box 1: P (no FTs), P1/P2/P3 (free throws), U=unsportsmanlike, D=disqualifying, T=technical, - = not committed by end, empty = blank.

foul5.description: Foul box 5. Fifth foul = fouled out, ejected. Same notation.

teamBPlayers.description: L2 player table (away / Team B). Same column order and rules as teamAPlayers.

bipinNo.description: Player licence / BIPIN / PIN (FIBA: Licence no. column).

playerInQuarter1.description: Player In Q1: X, /, or \ = played; empty = did not play.

playerInQuarter2.description: Player In Q2: X, /, or \ = played; empty = did not play.

playerInQuarter3.description: Player In Q3: X, /, or \ = played; empty = did not play.

playerInQuarter4.description: Player In Q4: X, /, or \ = played; empty = did not play.

foul1.description: Foul 1: P, P1, P2, P3, U, D, T, -, or empty.

foul5.description: Foul 5. Fifth foul = fouled out. Same notation.

runningScoreEvents.description: R1 running score. Inner columns 2–3 are point marks; outer 1 and 4 are Team A and Team B jersey numbers. Each scoring mark → one event; team A side only for L1/home; team B for L2/away. Ignore scribbled-out cells after the game. point = score sequence (1st basket, 2nd, …), not always the printed row number.

point.description: Scoring-play order (1st score of game = 1, …). FIBA sheet allows up to 160 plays (four 40-point blocks); domestic sheets often 120.

team.description: Scoring team: "A" = L1 / Team A side of the grid; "B" = L2 / Team B side. Never use 1, 2, L1, or L2 here.

type.description: 1 = free throw (dot in R1 point column), 2 = two-pointer (slash or backslash in R1 point column only; not player-row slashes), 3 = three-pointer (circle). Sum of type values for team A must match printed final for team A when every basket is captured.

periodScoresTeamA.description: Points scored by Team A in each period (1-4), from Scores section if visible.

periodScoresTeamB.description: Points scored by Team B in each period (1-4), from Scores section if visible.

finalScoreTeamA.description: Team A final score from the printed final on the sheet (authoritative for checking running-score types).

finalScoreTeamB.description: Team B final score from the printed final (authoritative for checking running-score types).

pointsPerColumn.description: R1: points per main column block (typically 40 on FIBA; four blocks ⇒ plays 1–160). Some sheets use 60 per block.

r2PeriodScoresTeamA.description: R2 – Period Scores: points scored by Team A in each period only (Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4), not cumulative. Omit if R2 not on sheet.

r2PeriodScoresTeamB.description: R2 – Period Scores: points scored by Team B in each period only. Omit if not on sheet.

r2ExtraPeriodPointsTeamA.description: FIBA Scores row: points scored by Team A in extra period(s) only (separate row 'Extra periods'). Use 0 if no overtime.

r2ExtraPeriodPointsTeamB.description: FIBA: extra-period points for Team B only. 0 if none.

r3FinalScoreTeamA.description: R3 – Final: Team A total final score (all periods and extra periods). Omit if R3 not on sheet.

r3FinalScoreTeamB.description: R3 – Final: Team B total final score. Omit if not on sheet.

r3WinningTeamName.description: R3 – Final: name of the winning team as written underneath the final score. Empty if not on sheet.
